changes.field: Stage 2B — Behavioural scope correctly defined, determinism risks surfaced
changes.finding: Behavioural scope is frontend rendering only: improving how existing governed DTO fields are displayed, suppressing duplicates, surfacing richer conditional content. Backend analytical reasoning, prose compilers, and scoring logic are explicitly forbidden. Determinism risk is conditional rendering on null/absent fields; the prompt correctly specifies 'missing content is omitted cleanly' at every phase — no fabrication or placeholder text permitted. No unintended analytical drift is possible within the permitted file set. Regression targets (8 named test files) are comprehensive.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md lines 251-255: Phase 4 rules — 'if explainer is absent, do not show placeholder text'

changes.field: next_steps[] field name vs next_steps_narrative string — field name ambiguity
changes.finding: The prompt references 'next_steps[]' as an array in Phase 5 (line 263). The frontend type contract (analysis.ts line 272) and page.tsx (line 217) show this surface is 'next_steps_narrative: string' — a single narrative string under narrative_report_v1, not an array. There is no 'next_steps[]' array field visible in the current type contract. If Cursor attempts to iterate 'next_steps[]' as an array it will fail. The Phase 1 STOP condition ('STOP if the current source...cannot be established') should catch this; Cursor should confirm the actual field shape during Phase 1 baseline inspection before implementing Phase 5.
changes.resolution: Advisory only — not a blocker. Cursor must confirm actual field shape during Phase 1 and use the correct field name. If next_steps[] does not exist as an array, use next_steps_narrative as the string source for the next-step section.

changes.resolution: Advisory only — implementation scope for Cursor, not a prompt defect.
